free_text: Investigation of the mechanism of inelastic deuteron
scattering on 12C at Ed = 15.3 MeV by the method of
d-gamma angular correlations
- English transl.
Carbon film with the thickness of 1.9 mg/cm2 was used
as a target.
Data were received from the authors
Entry was approved by authors.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
Subent 006 was split in three.
Data presented on fig. 1(a)
Angular dependencies of
differential cross section were measured in an
independent experiment by a single telescope
constructed from semiconductor detectors and arranged
outside the scattering chamber. The angular
resolution of the detector was +- 1 degr.
The error in the absolute values of the
cross sections was about 10% and was determined
primarily by the errors in estimating target thickness
and in calibrating the current integrator. The
uncertainty in target thickness was 5%.
No information about source of uncertainties
SD: ERR-ANALYS, DETECTOR updated. STATUS
moved to Subent 001. DATA-ERR1 -> ERR-1; DATA-ERR2 ->
DATA-ERR.

DATA-ERR.
No explanation of relative units is given.
Data presented on fig. 2
ANG-AZ-RL is an azimuthal angle between reaction
planes = 180.degr.
polar angle between beam and deuteron.
polar angle between beam and gamma-quantum.
Deuterons were detected by four surface barrier
silicon detectors with the thickness of sensitive
region up to 400 mu-m. The angular resolution of the
detectors was +-2 degr.
Photons were recorded by four BDEG-23
scintillation detectors of height and diameter 63 mm
that were based on NaI(TI) crystals and which were
arranged  at a fixed angular spacing of 32.5 degr. on a
moving horizontal ring platform outside the scattering
chamber. The angular resolution of gamma detectors
was +-13 degr.
Cross section errors were given by authors.
As a rule, statistical errors did not exceed 10 to 15%.
Data were presented by authors (fig.2).
SD: SF7=D/G -> G/D; SF8=NCP/REL -> REL in
REACTION code. ANG-AZ-RL=180.degr. excluded from the
COMMON section and values at ANG1 multiplied by -1.
STATUS moved to Subent 001. PART-DET, EN-SEC deleted.
ANG-SEC added. Part of data moved to Subents 007,008.
No explanation of relative units is given.
